heading: Compliance and accountability

children.heading: In general1So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.1
children.label: 1 In general1So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.1
A
children.text: the nutritional requirements of for school lunches; andsection 1758(f) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: as applicable, the nutritional requirements for school breakfasts under section 4(e)(1) of the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 ().42 U.S.C. 1773(e)(1)

children.label: A
children.text: require that local food service authorities comply with the nutritional requirements described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1);2So in original. Probably means subpars. (A) and (B) of par. (1) of subsec. (a).2

children.label: B
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, ensure compliance through reasonable audits and supervisory assistance reviews;

children.label: i
children.text: conduct audits and reviews during a 3-year cycle or other period prescribed by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: select schools for review in each local educational agency using criteria established by the Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: report the final results of the reviews to the public in the State in an accessible, easily understood manner in accordance with guidelines promulgated by the Secretary; and

children.label: iv
children.text: submit to the Secretary each year a report containing the results of the reviews in accordance with procedures developed by the Secretary; and

children.label: D
children.text: when any local food service authority is reviewed under this section, ensure that the final results of the review by the State educational agency are posted and otherwise made available to the public on request in an accessible, easily understood manner in accordance with guidelines promulgated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Minimization of additional duties
children.label: 2 Minimization of additional duties
children.text: Each State educational agency shall coordinate the compliance and accountability activities described in paragraph (1) in a manner that minimizes the imposition of additional duties on local food service authorities.

children.heading: Additional review requirement for selected local educational agencies
children.label: 3 Additional review requirement for selected local educational agencies

children.heading: Definition of selected local educational agencies
children.label: A Definition of selected local educational agencies
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “selected local educational agency” means a local educational agency that has a demonstrated high level of, or a high risk for, administrative error, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Additional administrative review
children.label: B Additional administrative review
children.text: In addition to any review required by subsection (a) or paragraph (1), each State educational agency shall conduct an administrative review of each selected local educational agency during the review cycle established under subsection (a).

children.heading: Scope of review
children.label: C Scope of review
children.text: In carrying out a review under subparagraph (B), a State educational agency shall only review the administrative processes of a selected local educational agency, including application, certification, verification, meal counting, and meal claiming procedures.

children.label: i
children.text: require the selected local educational agency to develop and carry out an approved plan of corrective action;

children.label: ii
children.text: except to the extent technical assistance is provided directly by the Secretary, provide technical assistance to assist the selected local educational agency in carrying out the corrective action plan; and

children.label: iii
children.text: conduct a followup review of the selected local educational agency under standards established by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), if the local educational agency fails to meet administrative performance criteria established by the Secretary in both an initial review and a followup review under paragraph (1) or (3) or subsection (a), the Secretary may require the State educational agency to retain funds that would otherwise be paid to the local educational agency for school meals programs under procedures prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: B Amount
children.text: The amount of funds retained under subparagraph (A) shall equal the value of any overpayment made to the local educational agency or school food authority as a result of an erroneous claim during the time period described in subparagraph (C).

children.label: i
children.text: beginning on the date the erroneous claim was made; and

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of the first followup review conducted by the State educational agency of the local educational agency under this section after , the date that is 60 days after the beginning of the period under clause (i); orJuly 1, 2005

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any subsequent followup review conducted by the State educational agency of the local educational agency under this section, the date that is 90 days after the beginning of the period under clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: to provide training and technical assistance related to administrative practices designed to improve program integrity and administrative accuracy in school meals programs to State educational agencies and, to the extent determined by the Secretary, to local educational agencies and school food authorities;

children.label: II
children.text: to assist State educational agencies in reviewing the administrative practices of local educational agencies in carrying out school meals programs; and

children.label: III
children.text: to carry out ; orsection 1769b–1(f) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: be credited to the child nutrition programs appropriation account.

children.heading: State share
children.label: B State share
children.text: A State educational agency may retain not more than 25 percent of an amount recovered under paragraph (4), to carry out school meals program integrity initiatives to assist local educational agencies and school food authorities that have repeatedly failed, as determined by the Secretary, to meet administrative performance criteria.

children.label: i
children.text: submit to the Secretary a plan describing how the State educational agency will use the funds to improve school meals program integrity, including measures to give priority to local educational agencies from which funds were retained under paragraph (4);

children.label: ii
children.text: consider using individuals who administer exemplary local food service programs in the provision of training and technical assistance; and

children.label: iii
children.text: obtain the approval of the Secretary for the plan.

children.heading: Eligibility determination review for selected local educational agencies
children.label: 6 Eligibility determination review for selected local educational agencies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A local educational agency that has demonstrated a high level of, or a high risk for, administrative error associated with certification, verification, and other administrative processes, as determined by the Secretary, shall ensure that the initial eligibility determination for each application is reviewed for accuracy prior to notifying a household of the eligibility or ineligibility of the household for free or reduced price meals.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be completed in a timely manner; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not result in the delay of an eligibility determination for more than 10 operating days after the date on which the application is submitted.

children.heading: Acceptable types of review
children.label: C Acceptable types of review
children.text: Subject to standards established by the Secretary, the system used to review eligibility determinations for accuracy shall be conducted by an individual or entity that did not make the initial eligibility determination.

children.heading: Notification of household
children.label: D Notification of household
children.text: Once the review of an eligibility determination has been completed under this paragraph, the household shall be notified immediately of the determination of eligibility or ineligibility for free or reduced price meals.

children.label: I
children.text: the number and percentage of reviewed applications for which the eligibility determination was changed and the type of change made; and

children.label: II
children.text: such other information as the Secretary determines to be necessary.

children.label: I
children.text: the number and percentage of reviewed applications for which the eligibility determination was changed and the type of change made; and

children.label: II
children.text: such other information as the Secretary determines to be necessary.

children.heading: Transparency
children.label: iii Transparency
children.text: The Secretary shall publish annually the results of the reviews of initial eligibility determinations by State, number, percentage, and type of error.
assist the State educational agency in the monitoring of programs conducted by local food service authorities; and
through management evaluations, review the compliance of the State educational agency and the local school food service authorities with regulations issued under this chapter.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated for purposes of carrying out the compliance and accountability activities referred to in subsection (c) $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2011 through 2015.

children.heading: Fines for violating program requirements
children.label: e Fines for violating program requirements
i
children.text: failed to correct severe mismanagement of the program;

children.label: ii
children.text: disregarded a program requirement of which the school food authority or school had been informed; or

children.label: iii
children.text: failed to correct repeated violations of program requirements.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In calculating the fine for a school food authority or school, the Secretary shall base the amount of the fine on the reimbursement earned by school food authority or school for the program in which the violation occurred.

children.label: I
children.text: 1 percent of the amount of meal reimbursements earned for the fiscal year for the first finding of 1 or more program violations under subparagraph (A);

children.label: II
children.text: 5 percent of the amount of meal reimbursements earned for the fiscal year for the second finding of 1 or more program violations under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: III
children.text: 10 percent of the amount of meal reimbursements earned for the fiscal year for the third or subsequent finding of 1 or more program violations under subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: failed to correct severe mismanagement of the program;

children.label: ii
children.text: disregarded a program requirement of which the State had been informed; or

children.label: iii
children.text: failed to correct repeated violations of program requirements.

children.label: i
children.text: 1 percent of funds made available under section 7(a) of the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 () for State administrative expenses during a fiscal year for the first finding of 1 or more program violations under subparagraph (A);42 U.S.C. 1776(a)

children.label: ii
children.text: 5 percent of funds made available under section 7(a) of the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 () for State administrative expenses during a fiscal year for the second finding of 1 or more program violations under subparagraph (A); and42 U.S.C. 1776(a)

children.label: iii
children.text: 10 percent of funds made available under section 7(a) of the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 () for State administrative expenses during a fiscal year for the third or subsequent finding of 1 or more program violations under subparagraph (A).42 U.S.C. 1776(a)

children.heading: Source of funding
children.label: 3 Source of funding
children.text: Funds to pay a fine imposed under paragraph (1) or (2) shall be derived from non-Federal sources.
